message:
refactor(agentmail): use full-path project slugs with legacy fallback and harden session storage

ProjectSlugFromPath now derives slugs from the entire absolute path
(e.g. "/Users/jemanuel/projects/ntm" -> "users-jemanuel-projects-ntm")
instead of just the basename ("ntm"). This eliminates slug collisions
when two projects share the same directory name but live at different
paths — a real problem in multi-agent environments where dozens of
sessions run concurrently against different project trees.

The previous basename-only logic is preserved as legacyProjectSlugFromPath
so that existing session data written under the old slug scheme is still
discoverable. HasArchiveForProject now probes both modern and legacy slug
directories. LoadSessionAgent and LoadSessionAgentRegistry iterate through
a prioritised list of slug candidates (modern full-path, legacy basename,
sanitized fallback) and skip any whose stored ProjectKey does not match
the requested one, continuing to the next candidate rather than returning
nil prematurely.

All session storage entry points (Load/Save/Delete for both SessionAgent
and SessionAgentRegistry) now validate the tmux session name via
validateSessionStorageName before constructing any filesystem path. This
prevents path-traversal attacks where a crafted session name like
"../escape" could read or write outside the sessions directory.

FetchInbox response parsing is tightened: explicit null responses and
null "result" fields now return descriptive errors instead of silently
producing empty slices, and a new schema_drift_is_error test confirms
that unexpected envelope shapes are rejected. The wrapper-then-direct
unmarshal cascade is preserved but each branch now surfaces the specific
parse failure rather than swallowing it.

Test coverage additions:
- slug_test.go expectations updated for full-path slug output
- session_fallback_test.go adds "Fallback to legacy slug path" and
  "Continue past mismatched candidate" scenarios
- session_test.go adds traversal-rejection tests for all six
  Save/Load/Delete operations across both SessionAgent and Registry
- client_test.go adds schema_drift_is_error case for FetchInbox

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,19 +7,19 @@ func TestProjectSlugFromPath(t *testing.T) {
 		input    string
 		expected string
 	}{
-		{"/Users/jemanuel/projects/ntm", "ntm"},
-		{"/home/user/code/my-project", "my-project"},
-		{"/var/www/html/site_v1", "site_v1"},
-		{"/tmp/test project", "test_project"},
-		{"/path/to/UPPERCASE", "uppercase"},
-		{"/path/to/mixed-CASE_project", "mixed-case_project"},
+		{"/Users/jemanuel/projects/ntm", "users-jemanuel-projects-ntm"},
+		{"/home/user/code/my-project", "home-user-code-my-project"},
+		{"/var/www/html/site_v1", "var-www-html-site_v1"},
+		{"/tmp/test project", "tmp-test-project"},
+		{"/path/to/UPPERCASE", "path-to-uppercase"},
+		{"/path/to/mixed-CASE_project", "path-to-mixed-case_project"},
 		{"/root", "root"},
 		{".", "root"},
 		{"/", "root"},
 		{"", ""},
-		{"/path/with/!@#$%^&*()", ""}, // All invalid chars
-		{"/path/to/valid-123_ok", "valid-123_ok"},
-		{"relative/path", "path"},
+		{"/path/with/!@#$%^&*()", "path-with"},
+		{"/path/to/valid-123_ok", "path-to-valid-123_ok"},
+		{"relative/path", "relative-path"},
 	}
 
 	for _, tt := range tests {
